mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an accommodative monetary policy that lowers borrowing costs and boosts equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods with declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 240) triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-inflation periods exceed 1.5% on average or Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust economic activity, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods with rising INDPRO/HOUST are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or pairwise correlation between INDPRO/HOUST < 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures that boost consumer spending and generate positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in low-unemployment periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS > 450,000) indicate resilient consumer demand, which drives corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-retail-sales periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or RSAFS growth decouples from SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in GDP (GDPC1 < 16400) signals economic stagnation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investor confidence declines.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDP contraction periods exceed -1% on average or Rank IC > 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150,000) indicates a robust labor market, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-payroll-growth periods are negative or PAYEMS growth decouples from SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising FEDFUNDS and declining CPIAUCSL signals a tightening cycle with disinflation, which reduces equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL co-moving periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or pairwise correlation < 0.2.
